payload.answer: Reject committing 500 row-level cards; preserve ambiguity and avoid durable storage of excessively granular, unsupported table atoms.

entity_resolution.reason: No stable user/person/entity facts were provided to resolve; only a validation fixture and request context are present.

payload.intent: Evaluate whether to persist 500 proposed memory cards from a large CSV table atomization request.

memory_cards.statement: Do not commit a large table atomized into hundreds of row-level memory cards when the content is not shown or is likely overly granular for durable memory.

repair_options: Summarize the table into a small number of durable higher-level facts if the rows are provided and clearly support them.
Store only a few clearly durable, non-overlapping facts instead of one memory per row.
If the rows contain preferences or identity facts, extract only the strongest stable items and leave the rest ephemeral.
